document.title: Extract line items from a small invoice PDF
document.description: Given an invoice PDF, return a JSON object with vendor, invoice_number, issue_date, and line_items[{description, qty, unit_price, total}].

rubric.signal: JSON contains all line items with correct totals; no fabricated fields

document.title: Summarize a 12-page research paper
document.description: Produce a 5-bullet summary of the paper's main claims, methodology, and limitations. Each bullet under 30 words.

rubric.signal: 5 bullets, each accurate, no hallucinated citations

document.title: Convert a multi-column PDF page to clean markdown
document.description: Given a page with 2 columns, footnotes, and a table, output well-structured markdown that preserves reading order, table structure, and footnote anchors.

rubric.signal: Reading order preserved; table renders as | pipes |; footnotes linked

document.title: Extract recipient address from a scanned letter
document.description: Pull the recipient's name, full street address, city, postal code, and country from a scanned letter image.

rubric.signal: All 5 fields correct or marked null if absent

document.title: Detect signature presence in a contract
document.description: Return JSON {signed: bool, signature_count: int, page_numbers: int[]} indicating whether the contract has signatures and on which pages.

rubric.signal: Booleans correct; pages match visible signatures

document.title: OCR a handwritten note
document.description: Transcribe a 3-line handwritten note. Preserve line breaks and any [unclear] markers for ambiguous words.

rubric.signal: Words match; ambiguous chars marked [unclear]

document.title: Extract a structured table from a financial report
document.description: From a financial report's income statement page, output the table as CSV with revenue, COGS, gross_profit rows for each year.

rubric.signal: CSV parses cleanly; numbers match source within rounding

document.title: Identify the language of a document
document.description: Return ISO 639-1 language code(s) detected in the document. Multi-lingual documents return all codes.

rubric.signal: Codes are valid ISO 639-1; covers all detected languages

document.title: Redact PII from a contract
document.description: Output the contract markdown with all personal names, emails, phone numbers, and SSNs replaced by [REDACTED-NAME], [REDACTED-EMAIL], etc.

rubric.signal: Zero PII leaks; non-PII text untouched

document.title: Generate a Q&A pair list from a manual
document.description: Given a 20-page user manual, generate 10 question/answer pairs covering the manual's main features. Each Q under 15 words.

rubric.signal: 10 pairs, answers grounded in source, no fabrication

document.title: Detect headings hierarchy
document.description: Output a JSON tree {h1: string, sections: [{h2: string, sections: [{h3: string}]}]} reflecting the document structure.

rubric.signal: Tree depth and sibling order match source

document.title: Compute total expense from a receipt
document.description: Given a receipt photo, return JSON {currency, items: [{name, price}], subtotal, tax, total}.

rubric.signal: Sum of items + tax = total within 0.01

sql.title: Count distinct users per day from a logs table
sql.description: Write a SQL query against `events(user_id uuid, occurred_at timestamptz, event_name text)` returning `(day, distinct_users)` for the last 30 days.

rubric.signal: Uses date_trunc, GROUP BY day, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id), filter on 30-day window

sql.title: Compute weekly retention cohorts
sql.description: From `events(user_id, occurred_at)`, compute weekly retention: % of users from cohort week W active in week W+N for N in [0..4].

rubric.signal: Uses cohort week + activity week + percentage; output pivot or long format

sql.title: Translate a Postgres query to BigQuery
sql.description: Convert a Postgres query that uses jsonb operators and date_trunc to equivalent BigQuery Standard SQL.

input_data.query: SELECT date_trunc('week', occurred_at) w, payload->>'plan' p, count(*) FROM events WHERE occurred_at > now() - interval '90 days' GROUP BY 1, 2

rubric.signal: Uses TIMESTAMP_TRUNC, JSON_VALUE, INTERVAL '90' DAY syntax for BigQuery

sql.title: Find duplicates by composite key
sql.description: Given `customers(email, phone, created_at)`, return rows where (email, phone) appears more than once. Show all duplicate rows, not just one per group.

rubric.signal: Uses window function or self-join correctly; returns full duplicate sets

sql.title: Join 3 tables with a date range filter
sql.description: Join `orders`, `users`, `products` to return order_id, user_email, product_name, total_amount for orders placed in Q1 2026.

rubric.signal: Two INNER JOINs, date range BETWEEN '2026-01-01' AND '2026-03-31'

sql.title: Optimize a slow query
sql.description: Given a query that does a full table scan on a 100M-row table, suggest indexes and rewrite the query to use them.

input_data.query: SELECT * FROM events WHERE event_name LIKE '%purchase%' AND occurred_at > '2026-01-01'

rubric.signal: Suggests index on (event_name, occurred_at), rewrites with = or prefix match if applicable

sql.title: Pivot rows to columns
sql.description: Convert a `metrics(day, metric_name, value)` table into one row per day with one column per metric_name.

rubric.signal: Uses FILTER (WHERE ...) inside aggregates or crosstab; preserves day per row

sql.title: Compute running total per group
sql.description: From `transactions(account_id, amount, occurred_at)`, return rows with a `running_balance` column per account.

rubric.signal: Uses SUM(...) OVER (PARTITION BY account_id ORDER BY occurred_at)

sql.title: Detect gaps in a sequence
sql.description: From `daily_metrics(day, value)`, identify days where data is missing within the min..max range.

rubric.signal: Uses generate_series anti-join or LAG to detect gaps

sql.title: Write a safe DELETE with rollback strategy
sql.description: Compose a DELETE statement that removes obsolete `sessions` (last_activity > 90 days ago) along with a verifying SELECT and a comment explaining why a transaction wrap is recommended.

rubric.signal: Includes BEGIN, SELECT count, DELETE, ROLLBACK/COMMIT semantics in commentary

data.title: Clean a messy CSV of customer records
data.description: Given a CSV with mixed-case emails, leading/trailing whitespace, inconsistent phone formats, return cleaned CSV with normalized fields.

rubric.signal: All emails lowercased; phones in E.164 or marked invalid; no surplus whitespace

data.title: Detect schema from a JSON sample
data.description: Given 50 JSON records with varying optional fields, output a schema spec listing each field, type, nullability, and presence rate.

rubric.signal: All fields enumerated; types correct; nullable flag matches the data

data.title: Compute summary statistics on a dataframe
data.description: From a CSV of 50k rows, return min/max/mean/median/std for each numeric column plus distinct-count for categorical columns.

rubric.signal: All columns covered; numbers match within rounding; categorical distinct counts correct

data.title: Pivot long to wide via pandas
data.description: Given a long table (`user_id, metric, value`), return wide pandas DataFrame with one row per user, metric columns.

rubric.signal: Resulting frame has expected shape; nulls fill correctly for missing metrics

data.title: Merge two CSVs with fuzzy join
data.description: Join two CSVs of company names where the names differ slightly ("Acme Inc." vs "acme, inc"). Return merged rows + similarity score.

rubric.signal: Uses normalization + similarity threshold; reports unmatched on each side

data.title: Aggregate logs into hourly buckets
data.description: From an NDJSON access log with timestamp + status_code + path, output hourly counts per status class (2xx/3xx/4xx/5xx).

rubric.signal: Hourly buckets; status classes summed; no off-by-one on hour edges

data.title: Detect outliers using IQR
data.description: On a numeric column from a CSV, flag values outside [Q1 - 1.5·IQR, Q3 + 1.5·IQR] and return the indices.

rubric.signal: IQR computed correctly; bounds correct; outlier indices match expected

data.title: Normalize a nested JSON to a flat table
data.description: Flatten nested JSON ({user: {profile: {name}}}) to flat columns user_profile_name. Handle arrays via row-explosion.

rubric.signal: Dot-path column names; array fields exploded into rows or marked

data.title: Build a percentile chart from a series
data.description: Given a list of latency_ms values, compute p50, p90, p95, p99, p99.9 and output as a small ASCII bar chart.

rubric.signal: Percentiles correct within 1ms; chart visually orders ascending

data.title: Validate a CSV against a schema
data.description: Given a schema spec and a CSV, return all rows that violate the schema, with the failed column and reason.

rubric.signal: All violations caught; no false positives; reason cites the rule

web.title: Extract product price from an Amazon-like listing
web.description: Given an HTML page, extract product title, price (with currency), availability, and image URL. Return JSON.

rubric.signal: All 4 fields present; price has currency; URL is absolute

web.title: Crawl a site for all article links
web.description: From a homepage, follow internal links one level deep and return a list of article URLs (filtering out nav/footer/social).

rubric.signal: Returns only article URLs; respects robots.txt; deduped

web.title: Submit a form and return the response page
web.description: Given a search form with one input, submit a query and return the structured results (title, url, snippet) from the resulting page.

rubric.signal: Submitted form correctly; results parsed into structured array

web.title: Scrape a paginated table
web.description: Given the first page of a paginated HTML table, follow next-page links and aggregate all rows into one CSV.

rubric.signal: All rows collected; pagination terminated correctly; CSV well-formed

web.title: Wait for dynamic content before reading
web.description: Page loads results via JS after page load. Wait until results are visible, then extract them.

rubric.signal: Waits on selector visibility before reading; doesn't read empty initial DOM

web.title: Detect login requirement
web.description: Determine whether a URL requires login. Return {requires_login: bool, evidence: string}.

rubric.signal: Boolean correct; evidence cites redirect to login or 401/403

web.title: Convert an HTML table to JSON
web.description: Parse an HTML <table> into JSON with column headers as keys.

rubric.signal: Headers detected; rows parsed; types kept as strings unless numeric coercion is requested

web.title: Extract OpenGraph metadata
web.description: From a URL, return {og:title, og:description, og:image, og:type} plus fallbacks to the regular <title> tag and meta description.

rubric.signal: All four fields with sensible fallbacks; image URL is absolute

web.title: Bypass a soft paywall via reader-view extraction
web.description: Detect a soft paywall and extract the article body using a reader-view algorithm (Mozilla Readability-style).

rubric.signal: Returns clean article text + author + publish date; no nav/ads

web.title: Take a screenshot of a specific element
web.description: Given a URL and a CSS selector, return a PNG screenshot of just that element.

rubric.signal: PNG bytes returned; image cropped to selector bounds

shell.title: Find all files larger than 100 MB
shell.description: Across a Unix filesystem, find all regular files >100MB and output them sorted by size desc.

rubric.signal: Uses find + size predicate or du; sorted descending; no symlink loops

shell.title: Tail a log file and filter ERROR lines in real-time
shell.description: Build a one-liner that tails /var/log/app.log and prints only lines containing ERROR with timestamp.

rubric.signal: Uses tail -F + grep; doesn't buffer (stdbuf or grep --line-buffered)

shell.title: Batch-rename PNG files to lowercase with timestamp prefix
shell.description: Rename all *.PNG to lowercase + prepend file mtime as YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-.

rubric.signal: Idempotent; no rename if already correctly formatted

shell.title: Compose a docker-compose for a Postgres + redis dev stack
shell.description: Output docker-compose.yml with Postgres 16, Redis 7, with volumes, healthchecks, and minimal env vars.

rubric.signal: Healthchecks defined; volumes named; depends_on with condition: service_healthy

shell.title: Find duplicate files by content hash
shell.description: Identify files with identical SHA-256 in a directory and group them.

rubric.signal: Uses find + xargs sha256sum + awk/sort; groups by hash

shell.title: Replace a string in all matching files
shell.description: Recursively replace foo with bar in *.js files under ./src, in-place, with backup.

rubric.signal: Uses find + sed -i with backup suffix; doesn't touch non-matching files

shell.title: Watch a port and restart a service on change
shell.description: Write a shell script that polls a port every 5s and restarts ./run.sh if the port is closed.

rubric.signal: Uses nc -z or ss; loops with sleep; restart only when port closed

shell.title: Extract HTTP latencies from an Nginx access log
shell.description: Parse Nginx logs and output p50/p95/p99 of upstream_response_time.

rubric.signal: Uses awk to extract field; computes percentiles; rounds sensibly

shell.title: Tar a directory excluding node_modules and .git
shell.description: Create a tar.gz of ./project excluding node_modules, .git, dist.

rubric.signal: Uses --exclude flags correctly; output verified by listing

shell.title: Grep all TODO comments and group by file
shell.description: Find all TODO/FIXME/HACK comments in a codebase and produce a markdown report grouped by file with line numbers.

rubric.signal: Markdown groups by file; line numbers included; no false positives in strings

code.title: Add comprehensive tests for a pure function
code.description: Given a JS function `calculatePriceWithTax(items, taxRate)`, write Jest tests covering: empty list, zero tax, mixed types, negative qty, large numbers.

input_data.function_code: function calculatePriceWithTax(items, taxRate) { return items.reduce((s, i) => s + i.price * i.qty, 0) * (1 + taxRate); }

rubric.signal: ≥6 test cases; covers edge cases listed; uses describe/it/expect

code.title: Refactor nested callbacks to async/await
code.description: Given a 3-level nested callback in Node.js, rewrite using async/await with proper try/catch.

input_data.code: fs.readFile('a.json', (e, a) => { if (e) return cb(e); fs.readFile('b.json', (e, b) => { if (e) return cb(e); cb(null, JSON.parse(a) + JSON.parse(b)); }); });

rubric.signal: Uses fs.promises; flat structure; errors propagate via try/catch

code.title: Find a bug in a sort comparator
code.description: A comparator returns true/false instead of -1/0/1, causing subtly wrong sort. Identify and fix.

rubric.signal: Identifies the truthy issue; rewrites as a.priority - b.priority; explains stability

code.title: Type a TypeScript generic function correctly
code.description: Add a fully-typed generic to `function pluck<T>(arr: T[], key: keyof T)`. Inferred return must be `T[keyof T][]`.

input_data.code: function pluck(arr, key) { return arr.map(x => x[key]); }

rubric.signal: Generic constraints correct; return type inferred without manual cast

code.title: Migrate a class component to React hooks
code.description: Convert a React class component with componentDidMount, componentDidUpdate, componentWillUnmount to a functional component using useEffect.

rubric.signal: Lifecycle correctly mapped; cleanup in unmount preserved; no extra rerenders

code.title: Add JSDoc types to a JS module
code.description: Annotate a 50-line JS module with full JSDoc types so TypeScript-server can type-check it.

rubric.signal: All exports typed; @param/@returns present; no any unless justified

code.title: Implement debounce in vanilla JS
code.description: Write a `debounce(fn, ms)` that delays calls and resets the timer on each new call. Include leading and trailing options.

rubric.signal: Implementation correct; leading/trailing options; respects this binding

code.title: Diagnose a memory leak in Node.js
code.description: A Node.js app's heap grows unbounded. Given a heap snapshot summary, identify the likely leak source and propose a fix.

input_data.snapshot_summary: Top retainer: EventEmitter listeners on db_pool

rubric.signal: Identifies missing removeListener / off; proposes cleanup pattern

code.title: Write a SQL injection-safe query builder fragment
code.description: Build a parameterized query for a search endpoint that filters by optional name, min_price, max_price.

rubric.signal: Uses $1/$2/$3 placeholders; never string-concats user input; handles missing optional filters

code.title: Implement a rate limiter middleware
code.description: Express middleware that enforces N requests/min/IP using a sliding window. Return 429 when exceeded.

rubric.signal: Sliding window algorithm correct; per-IP keys; 429 with Retry-After header

nextjs.title: Bootstrap a Next.js 15 App Router project with auth
nextjs.description: Produce a working scaffold: app/, server actions, a protected /dashboard route guarded by Supabase Auth GitHub OAuth.

rubric.signal: Runnable; uses RSC by default; protected route redirects when unauthed

nextjs.title: Convert a pages/ component to App Router with RSC
nextjs.description: Given a pages/posts/[slug].js with getServerSideProps, port to app/posts/[slug]/page.js as an async server component.

rubric.signal: No 'use client' unless interactive; data fetched in component body; no getServerSideProps

nextjs.title: Add a streaming Suspense boundary
nextjs.description: Wrap a slow data section in <Suspense> with a skeleton fallback in loading.js.

rubric.signal: loading.js created at the right segment; Suspense boundary in place; skeleton matches layout

nextjs.title: Set up dynamic OG images with next/og
nextjs.description: Create app/blog/[slug]/opengraph-image.js generating a 1200×630 PNG with the post title.

rubric.signal: Uses ImageResponse; size export correct; renders title from params

nextjs.title: Implement a middleware for locale detection
nextjs.description: middleware.ts redirects /foo to /<locale>/foo based on Accept-Language. Skip /api and static assets.

rubric.signal: Matcher excludes /api, /_next; redirect uses NextResponse.redirect

nextjs.title: Cache an external API call across requests
nextjs.description: Wrap a slow third-party fetch with React cache() + Next's revalidate to dedupe within a render and revalidate every 60s globally.

rubric.signal: Uses cache() for dedup; fetch with { next: { revalidate: 60 } }

nextjs.title: Build a server action for a contact form
nextjs.description: Server action that validates with zod, writes to Supabase, returns errors back via useFormState.

rubric.signal: 'use server' directive; zod schema; useFormState wired; redirect or revalidate on success

nextjs.title: Add a sitemap.js generator
nextjs.description: app/sitemap.js outputs static routes + dynamic blog posts pulled from a Supabase table.

rubric.signal: Returns array of {url, lastModified}; absolute URLs; dynamic data fetched once

react.title: Build a controlled multi-select with keyboard support
react.description: Accessible multi-select component: arrow nav, space to toggle, enter to confirm, esc to close.

rubric.signal: All keyboard interactions work; aria-expanded, aria-multiselectable correct

react.title: Memoize a heavy list render
react.description: A list with 5000 rows re-renders on every parent update. Use React.memo, useMemo, useCallback to fix without breaking semantics.

rubric.signal: Verifies via profiler; preserves stable identity for callbacks; no unnecessary re-renders

react.title: Add an error boundary that reports to Sentry
react.description: Class-based ErrorBoundary that catches render errors and reports to Sentry with component stack.

rubric.signal: Implements componentDidCatch; reports error + info; renders fallback UI

react.title: Convert a Redux slice to Zustand
react.description: Given a small Redux slice with sync + thunk actions, port to Zustand with same API surface for components.

rubric.signal: Components consume new store; thunks become async store actions; types preserved

react.title: Add Suspense for a data-fetching hook
react.description: Refactor a useEffect-based fetcher to throw a promise so it works with React.Suspense.

rubric.signal: Throws while pending; throws error on failure; resolves to data; cache by key

python-data.title: Profile a slow pandas pipeline
python-data.description: Given a pandas pipeline that takes 30s on 100k rows, profile and propose 3 concrete optimizations.

rubric.signal: Identifies real bottlenecks (apply→vectorize, dtype, groupby) with measurements

python-data.title: Write a parquet → DuckDB query pipeline
python-data.description: Read 5 parquet files, register them as DuckDB views, run a join + aggregation query.

rubric.signal: Uses duckdb.read_parquet or CREATE VIEW; query runs without materialising files

python-data.title: Build a simple feature pipeline with scikit-learn
python-data.description: Pipeline with imputation, OneHot, scaling, then a LogisticRegression. Include a 5-fold cross-val score.

rubric.signal: ColumnTransformer used; cross_val_score reported; no leakage between fit/transform

python-data.title: Plot a calibration curve
python-data.description: Given y_true and predicted probabilities, plot a calibration curve with 10 bins using matplotlib.

rubric.signal: Bins by predicted prob; expected vs observed plotted; diagonal reference shown

python-data.title: Ingest CSV to Postgres in chunks
python-data.description: Stream a 5GB CSV into Postgres without loading it all in memory. Use COPY or psycopg.copy.

rubric.signal: Uses COPY FROM STDIN; chunk size sane; txn wraps the whole load

backend-api.title: Design a paginated list endpoint
backend-api.description: REST endpoint GET /items with cursor pagination. Include the response schema and SQL.

rubric.signal: Cursor encoded as opaque token; ORDER BY id asc/desc; next_cursor returned

backend-api.title: Add JWT auth middleware
backend-api.description: Express/FastAPI middleware that validates a bearer JWT, attaches user to req, returns 401 on invalid.

rubric.signal: Verifies signature + exp + aud; req.user populated; 401 on missing/invalid

backend-api.title: Implement idempotency keys
backend-api.description: POST /payments uses Idempotency-Key header. Same key returns the original response without re-processing.

rubric.signal: Key stored with response; replays detected within TTL; partial failures handled

backend-api.title: Add request-id correlation
backend-api.description: Generate or propagate X-Request-Id, attach to logs, return on response.

rubric.signal: Middleware before logger; uuid v4 if missing; structured logs include the id

backend-api.title: Document an endpoint in OpenAPI
backend-api.description: Write the OpenAPI spec for POST /users with request body, response 201, error 400 + 409.

rubric.signal: Valid OpenAPI 3.1; schemas defined; examples present

mobile.title: Implement pull-to-refresh in a React Native FlatList
mobile.description: Add refreshControl with proper loading state and error toast.

rubric.signal: RefreshControl wired; refreshing state managed; error path shows toast

mobile.title: Persist navigation state across launches
mobile.description: Use @react-navigation/native + AsyncStorage to restore the user's last screen on relaunch.

rubric.signal: State serialized + restored; deep-link safe; no flicker on cold start

mobile.title: Wire a SwiftUI list to Combine fetcher
mobile.description: @StateObject ViewModel exposes @Published items; List binds; pull-to-refresh + error retry.

rubric.signal: MVVM separation clean; cancellables retained; refresh triggers fetch

mobile.title: Add Android background sync via WorkManager
mobile.description: Schedule a periodic sync every 6h with constraints (network, battery not low).

rubric.signal: PeriodicWorkRequest with constraints; unique work name; result handles retry

devops.title: Write a GitHub Actions workflow for a Node project
devops.description: Run lint + test on every PR + main push. Cache npm. Matrix on Node 20/22.

rubric.signal: actions/setup-node with cache: npm; matrix matrix.node-version; concurrency cancel-in-progress

devops.title: Author a multi-stage Dockerfile for a Next.js app
devops.description: Builder stage installs + builds. Runtime stage copies standalone output, runs as non-root.

rubric.signal: Two stages; standalone output used; runs as non-root user; .dockerignore mentioned

devops.title: Set up a Terraform module for an S3 bucket
devops.description: Module that creates a private bucket with versioning + lifecycle rule moving to IA after 30d.

rubric.signal: aws_s3_bucket + versioning + lifecycle; public access block on; tags pass-through

devops.title: Write a kubectl rollout command sequence
devops.description: Roll out a new image, watch the rollout, rollback if it fails health checks within 5 minutes.

rubric.signal: kubectl set image, rollout status with timeout, rollout undo on fail

devops.title: Configure structured logging with correlation
devops.description: App logs JSON to stdout with trace_id; log shipper (Vector/Fluentbit) example included.

rubric.signal: JSON fields stable; trace_id propagated; example shipper config included

ml-training.title: Implement gradient checkpointing in PyTorch
ml-training.description: Wrap a transformer block to use torch.utils.checkpoint to reduce activation memory.

rubric.signal: Uses checkpoint correctly; no double-counting; verified by memory drop

ml-training.title: Set up DDP for multi-GPU training

rubric.signal: init_process_group + DDP wrap; sampler set_epoch; no hang on shutdown

ml-training.description: Log metrics, hyperparams, model artifact to Weights & Biases; resume on rerun.

ml-training.title: Build a simple LR scheduler with warmup
ml-training.description: Linear warmup for 10% of steps then cosine decay to 0.

generic.title: Document the project's mental model
generic.description: Produce a 1-page README that explains the project's purpose, the tech stack, the boundary between server and client, and the way to add a new feature.

generic.title: Set up Husky + lint-staged

rubric.signal: Husky pre-commit hook installed; lint-staged config sane; bypass instructions documented

generic.title: Write a CONTRIBUTING.md
generic.description: Cover dev setup, commit conventions, branch naming, PR requirements, code of conduct pointer.

rubric.signal: All five sections present; tone matches an OSS project

generic.title: Add an .editorconfig + Prettier baseline
